name: Sablefish
desc: A prized deep-sea fish known for its buttery flavor and high oil content, often called 'Black Cod'.

name: Brotula
desc: A slow-moving, eel-like fish found in deep benthic environments throughout the Pacific.

name: Pacific Hagfish
desc: An ancient scavenger famous for producing massive amounts of defensive slime when threatened.

name: Rex Sole
desc: A small-mouthed flatfish that inhabits the soft muddy bottoms of the continental shelf.

name: Sculpin
desc: Bottom-dwelling fish known for their large, spiny heads and excellent camouflage.

name: Pacific Hake
desc: A key migratory species in the Pacific ecosystem, often found in large schools at mid-depths.

name: Dover Sole
desc: A long-lived flatfish that can survive in low-oxygen environments deep on the ocean floor.

name: Rattail
desc: Deep-sea scavengers characterized by large eyes and a long, tapering tail used for slow cruising.

name: Catshark
desc: Small, ground-dwelling sharks named for their distinctive elongated, cat-like eyes.

name: Rockfish
desc: A incredibly diverse genus of long-lived fish known for their bright colors and venomous dorsal spines.

name: Shortspine Thornyhead
desc: A deep-living rockfish that spends most of its time resting motionlessly on the seabed.

name: Combfish
desc: Slender fish with rough, comb-like scales that live among the sand and cobble of the shelf.

name: Poacher Fish
desc: Small, armored fish covered in bony plates that protect them from predators on the sea floor.

name: Skate
desc: Flat, cartilaginous fish that glide over the bottom and lay distinct leathery egg cases.

name: Eelpout
desc: Elongated, bottom-dwelling fish that inhabit cold, deep waters across the North Pacific.

name: Duckbill Eel
desc: An eel species easily identified by its exceptionally long, slender, duck-like snout.

name: Flatfish
desc: A broad order of fish that lie flat on the ocean floor with both eyes located on one side of their head.

name: Argentine
desc: Small, silvery fish often found near the edges of the continental shelf.

name: Pacific Sanddab
desc: A small flatfish that perfectly mimics the color and texture of the sandy bottom it rests on.

name: Buffalo Sculpin
desc: A robust sculpin with large, defensive spines and a heavy, buffalo-like profile.

name: Chimaera
desc: Also called 'Ratfish,' these are ancient relatives of sharks with a smooth, ghost-like appearance.

name: Eelpout
desc: A slender genus of eelpout found in the deep, cold canyons of the continental slope.

name: Grenadier
desc: Known as 'Rattails,' these are among the most abundant and important scavengers in the deep sea.

name: Lanternfish
desc: Small, bioluminescent fish that migrate to the surface at night to feed.

name: Cusk Eel
desc: Eel-like fish known for their ability to produce sound using specialized muscles and their swim bladder.

name: English Sole
desc: A common flatfish in the Pacific Northwest, distinguished by its pointed snout and slender body.

name: Turbot
desc: Flatfish with high-set eyes and mottled skin that allows them to vanish into gravelly sea floors.

name: Midshipman Fish
desc: Famous for the low-frequency 'hum' males produce to attract mates during the summer months.

name: Blacktip Poacher
desc: A tiny, armored fish with distinctive black tips on its fins, typically found in sandy habitats.

name: Pink Seaperch
desc: A small, brightly colored perch found in deep temperate waters off the western coast.
